places.name: Mapparium Globe
places.description: An enormous, inside-out glass globe built in 1935.

places.name: Brattle Book Shop
places.description: One of the oldest used bookstores in the U.S. has been selling antiquarian treasures since 1825.

places.name: Forest Hills Cemetery
places.description: A beautiful Victorian-era cemetery, complete with a miniature village.

places.name: Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum (The Gardner)
places.description: Two thousand artifacts from around the world collected by one woman who loved to travel.

places.name: Edgar Allan Poe Square
places.description: The Boston square dedicated to the dark poet who was born nearby.

places.name: Caffe Vittoria
places.description: The oldest Italian café in Boston, this spot also serves as a veritable museum of vintage coffee ephemera.

places.name: Boston Athenaeum Skin Book
places.description: A morbid secret lies hidden within the beautiful walls of the Boston Athenaeum.

places.name: Empire Garden Restaurant
places.description: Dim sum, served in a grand old theater.

places.name: Great Boston Molasses Flood Plaque
places.description: The site of one of the strangest disasters in history—a wave of deadly molasses traveling at 35 mph.
